title: Chicken Cacciatore

overview.description: Rustic Italian braised chicken cooked slowly with tomatoes, peppers, onions, mushrooms and herbs.

method: Season the chicken thighs generously with salt and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-based pan or casserole over a medium-high heat.
Brown the chicken thighs skin-side down until the skin is golden and crisp.
Turn and brown the other side briefly.
Remove the chicken from the pan.
Add the onion and pepper to the same pan.
Add the garlic, anchovies and mushrooms.
Pour in the white wine and scrape the bottom of the pan to release any browned bits.
Allow the wine to reduce by approximately half.
Add the chopped tomatoes, chicken stock, oregano, rosemary and bay leaves.
Return the chicken to the pan, skin-side up.
Cover and cook gently for 35–45 minutes until the chicken is tender and cooked through.
Stir in the olives and capers during the final 10 minutes.
Scatter with fresh parsley before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Cacciatore means 'hunter-style' in Italian, referring to rustic dishes traditionally made with whatever ingredients were available.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How browning meat, deglazing a pan and slowly braising ingredients create a deeply flavoured one-pot dish.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A rustic, deeply flavoured chicken dish that rewards slow cooking and is practical enough to make as a larger batch.
chapman-tips: Brown the chicken properly before adding the sauce. This is where much of the flavour begins.
Deglaze the pan thoroughly to incorporate the browned bits into the sauce.
Keep the skin above the sauce if you want it to retain some texture.
The sauce should be thick enough to coat the chicken rather than watery.
Chicken thighs are more forgiving than breast meat during a long braise.
notes.batch-cooking: Allow the cooked dish to cool completely before freezing in portions. Freeze the chicken and sauce together.
notes.leftovers: Leftover chicken can be shredded into the sauce and served with pasta, rice or crusty bread.
